# Granzyme B

## Summary

Granzyme B is the strongest functional readout in the current IL-42 suppression paper. It behaves as a more sensitive indicator of reduced CD8 effector capacity than CD69 in this source.

## Key Findings

- [supports] IL-42 reduced granzyme B-positive activated primary CD8 cells by about 38 percent in the 24-hour assay. [[lee-2026-il42-cd8-suppression]]
- [supports] Jurkat cells showed a weaker granzyme B reduction of about 15 percent, which suggests context-dependent magnitude. [[lee-2026-il42-cd8-suppression]]
- [extends] Anti-IL-42 neutralization restored granzyme B toward baseline, strengthening the causal link between IL-42 exposure and reduced granzyme B output. [[lee-2026-il42-cd8-suppression]]

## Contradictions and Caveats

- Granzyme B was measured at one time point only.
- The paper does not show whether the decrease reflects transcriptional repression, reduced activation state, or another upstream mechanism.

## Open Questions

- Is granzyme B the best readout for IL-42 across other CD8 models?
- Does IL-42 also affect perforin or other cytotoxic markers in parallel?
